questions.question: How do rays parallel to the principal axis behave after reflection from a convex mirror?
questions.option_1: They appear to diverge from a point on the principal axis.
questions.option_2: They get reflected back along the same path.
questions.option_3: They pass through the centre of curvature.
questions.option_4: They actually converge at the focus.

questions.question: How does the magnetic field strength at the center of a circular loop change if the current through the loop is doubled?
questions.option_1: It becomes half as large
questions.option_2: It becomes twice as large
questions.option_4: It becomes four times as large

questions.question: If a constant force acts on two objects of different masses, which statement best describes their acceleration according to the second law of motion?
questions.option_1: The object with greater mass will have greater acceleration.
questions.option_2: Acceleration does not depend on mass.
questions.option_3: The object with smaller mass will have greater acceleration.
questions.option_4: Both objects will have the same acceleration.

questions.question: If a convex mirror forms a point-sized image at its focus, where is the object located?
questions.option_1: At the pole
questions.option_2: Between P and F
questions.option_4: Between F and the pole

questions.question: If a material has an absolute refractive index of 1.7, what does this imply about the speed of light in that material compared to vacuum?
questions.option_1: Light travels at the same speed in both.
questions.option_2: Light travels 1.7 times faster in the material than in vacuum.
questions.option_3: The speed difference cannot be determined from this value.
questions.option_4: Light travels 1.7 times slower in the material than in vacuum.

questions.question: If a point is located below the principal axis of a spherical mirror, what sign is assigned to its perpendicular distance according to the sign convention for spherical mirrors?
questions.option_1: Depends on mirror type
questions.option_2: Negative
questions.option_4: Zero

questions.question: If an ammeter reading increases when a thicker wire replaces a thinner one of the same material and length, what is the most likely reason?
questions.option_1: The voltage across the wire decreased
questions.option_2: The resistance of the thicker wire is lower, allowing more current
questions.option_4: The wire's resistivity increased

questions.question: If atmospheric refraction did not occur, which change would be most noticeable during the observation of stars from Earth?
questions.option_1: Stars would change color rapidly.
questions.option_2: Stars would disappear from the night sky.
questions.option_4: Stars would not twinkle; their light would remain steady.

questions.question: If the current in a wire is 2 amperes, how much charge flows through a cross-section in 5 seconds?
questions.option_1: 7 coulombs
questions.option_2: 10 coulombs
questions.option_4: 0.4 coulombs

questions.question: If two cars start at the same point and move along a straight road, Car X at a constant speed of 40 km/h and Car Y at a constant speed of 60 km/h, which statement correctly compares their motions after 2 hours?
questions.option_1: Car Y will be 20 km ahead of Car X after 2 hours.
questions.option_2: Both cars will have traveled the same distance after 2 hours.
questions.option_3: Car X will be 40 km ahead of Car Y after 2 hours.
questions.option_4: Car Y will be 40 km ahead of Car X after 2 hours.

questions.question: In a parallel combination of three resistors, which statement correctly describes the total current in the circuit?
questions.option_1: It is the average of the currents through each resistor
questions.option_2: It is equal to the sum of the currents through each resistor
questions.option_3: It is the same as the current through each resistor
questions.option_4: It is equal to the current through the resistor with the lowest resistance

questions.question: In the context of uniform circular motion, which of the following examples from everyday life best illustrates this concept?
questions.option_1: A car moving with changing speed along a circular track
questions.option_2: A person walking randomly in a park
questions.option_3: A satellite revolving around the Earth at a fixed speed in a circular orbit
questions.option_4: A pendulum bob swinging to and fro

questions.question: Ozone shields the surface of the Earth from which type of radiation coming from the Sun?
questions.option_1: White light
questions.option_2: X rays
questions.option_4: Ultra violet

questions.question: Suppose a student wants to design an amusement park ride that moves people in a circle at uniform speed. Which concept should the student apply to ensure riders stay safely in their seats during the circular motion?
questions.option_1: Increasing speed randomly will keep them safe
questions.option_2: A constant outward force is needed to keep riders pressed against their seats
questions.option_3: A constant inward (centripetal) force is needed on the riders to keep them in uniform circular motion
questions.option_4: Gravity alone is sufficient to keep the riders in place

questions.question: What does Snell's law of refraction state about the ratio of the sine of the angle of incidence to the sine of the angle of refraction for a given colour and pair of media?
questions.option_1: It depends on the type of mirror.
questions.option_2: It varies with the normal.
questions.option_3: It changes with the angle of incidence.
questions.option_4: It remains constant.

questions.question: What happens to the potential energy of an object if its mass is halved while its height above the ground remains constant?
questions.option_1: The potential energy becomes zero
questions.option_2: The potential energy is halved
questions.option_4: The potential energy is doubled

questions.question: What happens to the work done if the displacement becomes zero despite a force being applied?
questions.option_1: The work done remains constant.
questions.option_2: The work done decreases.
questions.option_4: The work done is zero.

questions.question: What is the reason why electrons move in a conducting wire when connected to a battery, but not when the wire is left untouched?
questions.option_1: A battery creates a potential difference that sets electrons in motion
questions.option_2: Gravity acts on electrons in the wire
questions.option_4: Electrons have intrinsic energy for movement

questions.question: What is the sign of the focal length for a convex lens as per the sign convention for spherical lenses?
questions.option_1: Positive
questions.option_2: Negative
questions.option_4: Variable

questions.question: What role does the earth wire play in the safety of domestic appliances with metallic bodies?
questions.option_1: It prevents the fuse from melting
questions.option_2: It controls the current flow
questions.option_4: It provides a low-resistance path for leakage current

questions.question: When a gun is fired, it recoils backward as the bullet moves forward. How does this demonstrate Newton’s third law of motion?
questions.option_1: The gun recoils because the bullet moves faster than the gun.
questions.option_2: The bullet moves forward only because the gun has stored energy.
questions.option_3: The gun pushes the bullet forward, and the bullet pushes the gun backward with an equal and opposite force.
questions.option_4: The gun moves backward because it is heavier than the bullet.

questions.question: When a light ray enters a rectangular glass slab obliquely from air, what happens to its direction at the first interface?
questions.option_1: It gets reflected back
questions.option_2: It bends away from the normal
questions.option_4: It bends towards the normal

questions.question: When a man pushes a wall but the wall does not move, what is the work done by the man on the wall?
questions.option_1: Maximum work is done as the man uses full effort.
questions.option_2: Zero work is done as there is no displacement of the wall.
questions.option_3: Positive work is done as force is applied.
questions.option_4: Negative work is done as the wall resists the push.

questions.question: When an object is in free fall near the Earth's surface, which of the following physical quantities remains constant if air resistance is neglected?
questions.option_1: Momentum remains constant.
questions.option_2: Velocity remains constant.
questions.option_4: Acceleration due to gravity remains constant.

questions.question: When an object is placed at infinity in front of a convex mirror, what is the nature of the image formed?
questions.option_1: Real and inverted
questions.option_2: Real and erect
questions.option_4: Virtual and erect
